comment: Baseline of SHIPPED def_id (T0327 И2c; lead-ratified 2026-07-07: deleting/renaming a shipped def_id is destructive and must FORCE an explicit developer reaction). See src/features/items/README.md 'Lock workflow' for the full step-by-step. Two sections: `def_ids` = ids currently live and shipped (append-only -- once here, must stay in the catalog until deliberately moved to `removed`). `removed` = ids DELIBERATELY removed from the catalog after shipping, keyed by def_id, each entry `{fragment_version, note}`: `fragment_version` (required, integer >= 2) records WHICH state/items.schema.json version bump + migration step handled the removal, and must be <= the CURRENT items fragment version (i.e. actually delivered, not just declared); `note` is optional free-text documentation, not checked. Many removals can share the SAME fragment_version -- one release can batch several deletions into one version bump + one migration step (e.g. delete 10 defs -> all 10 get one shared fragment_version in `removed` -> one migration step handles all of them). A shipped id vanishing from items.json without a matching `removed` entry fails `items_ops.py validate` with removed-without-reaction. An id restored to the catalog after being in `removed` is legal (warning only, removed-def-restored) -- move it back to `def_ids` and drop the `removed` entry once confirmed permanent.
